text: Writing clean code is essential for any developer who wants their programs to be easy to understand and modify. The most important quality of clean code is readability. When your code is readable, you and others can quickly grasp what it does. One simple way to improve readability is to follow good naming conventions. Instead of using vague names like `x` or `data`, choose clear, descriptive names such as `totalPrice` or `userEmail`. Another key practice is to break your code into small, focused functions. Each function should do just one thing. This makes your code more organised and easier to test. By writing clean code, you ensure its long-term maintainability, saving time and effort when updates are needed later.
